text:
There used to be an old bookstore on a cobblestone street in old town Cambridge. Every evening the books murmured secrets to their quiet, wise keeper.

One chilly October evening, Emma visited just to hear the gossip about old Mr. Fluke.

Mr. Fluke had been famous for bringing out the biggest racoons. They'd gather at his shop after-dark, roasting worms on sticks.

"Mr. Fluke always says racoons have the most beautiful voices," the owl perched on an owl hook whispered.

Emma laughed. "You old owl!"

"Aww, I'm just fond." The owl fluttered its wings playfully.

As she browsed, a faint melody hummed through the shelves. The old clock ticked.

Emma followed its tune to an antique flute.

"Oh, how sweet," the owl said. "You'd think those old feathers could play."

Emma found Mr. Fluke playing. His fingers danced across the flute.

"Those little wings just mimic
